user_query:
I keep a personal K-drama watch log and want to turn it into something I can share with friends (without spoilers). Using input/drama_watchlog.csv, please do the following and save all outputs under the output/ directory:

1) Filter to only rows where country == "South Korea".

2) Compute and save output/genre_stats.csv with columns: genre, dramas_count, avg_rating, total_hours. 
   - dramas_count: number of South Korean titles in each genre.
   - avg_rating: mean of my_rating for that genre, rounded to 2 decimals.
   - total_hours: sum over episodes * minutes_per_episode / 60 for that genre, rounded to 1 decimal.

3) Compute and save output/platform_stats.csv with columns: platform, dramas_count, avg_rating, total_hours. 
   - Same definitions and rounding rules as in (2), grouped by platform.

4) Create output/top5_kdramas.csv listing my top 5 South Korean titles by my_rating (desc). 
   - Tie-breakers: higher total_hours first, then title alphabetically.
   - Columns: rank (1–5), title, my_rating, genre, total_hours (rounded to 1 decimal).

5) Rewrite my casual notes for each South Korean title to be shareable with friends (no spoilers). Save as output/rephrased_notes.csv with columns: title, original_note, rewritten_note.
   - rewritten_note must be in a friendly, neutral tone, concise (at most 2 sentences), and <= 180 characters.
   - Do not reveal plot twists, endings, or specific spoiler details.

Assume the output/ directory may not exist and create it if needed. Use only the provided CSV as input. Thanks! I’m not from Korea, but I adore K-dramas and want stats plus friend-safe notes. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
